name: Sea Bass with Citrus Couscous
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Red onions - sliced (lengthwise 1/4 inch thick)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------COUSCOUS AND FISH-----------------------------
3 cups Vegetable stock
10 ounces Instant couscous
Zest of 1 orange - grated
Zest of 1 lemon - grated
Salt and pepper
Zest of 1 lime - grated
1/4 teaspoon Asian chili paste
SAUCE: Heat the olive oil in a nonreactive medium saucepan. Add the onions and cook over moderately low heat, stirring, until softened, about 8 minutes. Add the orange juice and cook over moderately high heat until reduced by half, about 8 minutes. Stir in the parsley, salt, pepper, coriander, and Tabasco; keep warm.
COUSCOUS AND FISH: In a nonreactive medium saucepan, combine the stock, citrus zests (orange, lemon, lime), chili paste, and 1 tablespoon of oil. Bring to a boil over high heat. Stir in the couscous, cover, and let stand off the heat until liquid is absorbed, about 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and fluff with a fork.
Light grill or preheat broiler. Rub the sea bass with the remaining tablespoon of oil and season on both sides with salt and pepper. Grill or broil for about 2 minutes, or until lightly golden and sizzling. Gently turn the fish, drizzle with lemon juice, and broil for about 3 more minutes, or until just cooked through.
Divide the couscous among 6 plates. Arrange the sea bass on the couscous, spoon the orange sauce over, and garnish with extra parsley sprigs.
